reason: Bitcoin is showing strong signals of whale distribution into retail buying, suggesting the recent dip may not be over and further downside is possible. This is exacerbated by general altcoin weakness and significant regulatory uncertainty from new U.S. tax-reporting rules. While the growth in tokenized assets is a long-term positive, it does not outweigh the immediate bearish technical and regulatory pressures.
headlines: Tokenized assets exceed $25 billion after nearly quadrupling in a year
Canton’s Yuval Rooz says smart contract blockchains face a reckoning over value gap
Bitcoin dip may not be over as whales sell into retail buying — a bearish signal
XRP slips as traders watch $1.35 support
Coinbase says new U.S. tax-reporting rules for crypto are cluttered, confusing
